message:
feat(clusters): add Remove cluster button for offline clusters (#5901) (#5908)

Surface the backend /kubeconfig/remove endpoint (added in #5658) in the
cluster UI. Offline / unreachable cluster cards now show a trash button
that opens a confirmation dialog and, on confirm, calls the local agent
to delete the stale kubeconfig context and refreshes the list.

The button only renders when:
- The local agent is connected
- The cluster is unreachable (reachable === false)
- The cluster came from the kubeconfig source

Includes a new RemoveClusterDialog modal, i18n keys across all 9 locales,
and wiring through Grid/List/Compact card variants.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,111 @@ +import { useState } from 'react' +import { Loader2, Trash2, AlertTriangle } from 'lucide-react' +import { useTranslation } from 'react-i18next' +import { BaseModal } from '../../../lib/modals' + +interface RemoveClusterDialogProps { + isOpen?: boolean + /** Context name as stored in kubeconfig (what the backend needs) */ + contextName: string + /** Friendly display name shown to the user */ + displayName: string + onClose: () => void + /** Resolves on success, rejects with an Error on failure */ + onConfirm: (contextName: string) => Promise +} + +/** + * Confirmation dialog for removing an offline cluster's kubeconfig context. + * + * Implements issue #5901: the backend endpoint for removing a stale kubeconfig + * context (`/kubeconfig/remove`) was added in #5658 but there was no UI affordance + * to invoke it. This dialog is surfaced from the cluster card's "Remove cluster" + * button, which only appears for unreachable clusters. + */ +export function RemoveClusterDialog({ + isOpen = true, + contextName, + displayName, + onClose, + onConfirm }: RemoveClusterDialogProps) { + const { t } = useTranslation() + const [status, setStatus] = useState<{ removing: boolean; error: string | null }>({ + removing: false, + error: null }) + + const handleConfirm = async () => { + setStatus({ removing: true, error: null }) + try { + await onConfirm(contextName) + onClose() + } catch (err) { + setStatus({ + removing: false, + error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : t('cluster.removeClusterError') }) + } + } + + return ( + + + + +
+ +

+ {t('cluster.removeClusterWarning')} +

+
+ +

+ {t('cluster.removeClusterContext')} +

+

{displayName}

+ + {status.error && ( +

+ {status.error} +

+ )} + +

{t('cluster.removeClusterDesc')}

+ + + +
+
+ + +
+ + + ) +}
